item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fascinating article! The realistic nature of murderous birds is one that truly terrifies me, which is a great sign! I also love how in depth the article is. You answer many possible questions, while still leaving some part vague enough for theorizing. You also did a very good job of separating this article from other objects that produce anomalies, such as SCP-914. All in. all, very intriguing article! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm surprised nobody commented on this yet. I, personally, love this! And not because of the setting or anything ;)

I do enjoy the sheer depth you've put into this article. That depth is present in some of your other, similar articles — such as SCP-4248 — and I really do think it enriches the surrounding universe. This is also thoroughly enjoyable as a prequel/side-quel to SCP-3095, which is why I was a bit confused that you chose not to make an explicit link to Team Bird here (other articles, such as SCP-4688, are even less connected, yet appear in the series nonetheless).

I digress; it's good to see this on the site. Hearty +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
TEAM BIRD BE- well, technically, this isn't a Team Bird SCP. It's sort of a prequel that has nothing to do with it. My main objective here was to try to worldbuild with GoI-301 from SCP-3095.

Thanks to FloppyPhoenix for helping me brainstorm this. Thank you to Mew-ltiverse, Laneous, DeadAndCuddly and someone whose name I'm blanking on for critique.

Image source:https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Central_Altar_(2854739802).jpg
